projects.name: Clearstack
projects.description: A no-build web component framework specification — and its working proof — built entirely through LLM-human collaboration. ES modules served directly to the browser, ≤150 lines per file, JSDoc over TypeScript, and a spec checker that enforces its own rules.
projects.background: Born from frustration with modern frontend tooling that optimizes for machines over humans. If every file is small, explicit, and runs as authored — both humans and LLMs can reason about it with zero friction.

projects.name: StatiCart
projects.description: A full-featured e-commerce platform built 99% on static hosting. Stripe-powered checkout, DynamoDB single-table backend, Lambda API, GitHub Actions stock-triggered rebuilds — all scaffolded via Clearstack.
projects.background: What if an entire storefront could live on a CDN with no server runtime costs? StatiCart proves it's possible with modern JAMstack patterns and a minimal serverless backend.

projects.name: Asili
projects.description: Privacy-first polygenic risk score analysis. Upload your DNA, get trait scores — all processing happens on your device via DuckDB WASM. No accounts, no servers, no data leaves your browser.
projects.background: Genomic data is intensely personal. Asili proves you can deliver meaningful health insights without ever touching a user's data — validated across 3 individuals × 647 traits.

projects.name: ThinClock
projects.description: Custom firmware for the Ulanzi TC001 LED matrix. The device is intentionally dumb (fetches JSON, renders pixels) while a composable Node.js server handles all the logic — weather, Home Assistant, alerts, particles, and animations.
projects.background: A smart display that's actually dumb by design. All intelligence lives server-side, making the firmware trivial to update and the display infinitely configurable.
